slides.summary:
Generating Long Documents
• AI can be used to generate long, coherent 
text documents
• Books, reports, screenplays
• Today we will build an agentic flow to 
generate screenplays for movies
• This flow architecture can be generalized to 
other long text documents

slides.relation_to_previous: This slide extends the prior discussion anchored in “Slide 1”, carrying forward the ideas around: AI Screenplays.

slides.summary:
Challenge: "One-Shot" 
Long-Form
• It is difficult for an AI to generate a long 
coherent document in one-shot
• Local vs global – the AI is good at writing 
small parts, but cannot maintain coherence 
across whole document
– Becomes “lost in the middle”
– Context drift – deep into the document, the AI can 
hallucinate or lose focus of what to write
bullet_points: • It is difficult for an AI to generate a long
• Local vs global – the AI is good at writing
small parts, but cannot maintain coherence

slides.summary:
Movie Screenplays
• Screenplays have many different elements 
the AI needs to capture
– Story shape
– Goals of “sequences” in the movie
– Scene action and dialogue
• Hierarchical structure of a screenplay
– Premise – basic elements of the movie
– Story arc – high level plot structure
– Sequences - more detailed plot
– Scenes – even more detailed plot

slides.summary:
Premise Agent
• A chat agent that discusses with you what your 
movie is about
– Title, logline (like an executive summary), characters, genre, 
central conflict
• You can ask the agent to give ideas for the plot if 
you are stuck
• This chat can take a few minutes even if you have a 
general sense about your movie (takes time to 
specify all the major elements of the movie)
• This agent then creates a file premise.json

slides.summary:
Arc Agent
• A chat agent that discusses your movie story arc
– Arc consists of multiple acts
– Agent takes premise.json as input for context
• You tell the agent what happens in each act, then 
ask it to draft the act, one at time
• Premise file gives it context for the story arc
• This agent then creates a file arc.json
bullet_points: • A chat agent that discusses your movie story arc
– Agent takes premise.json as input for context
• You tell the agent what happens in each act, then
ask it to draft the act, one at time
slides.relation_to_previous: This slide extends the prior discussion anchored in “Slide 9”, carrying forward the ideas around: Premise.json.

ask it.

slides.summary:
Sequence Agent
• An automated agent (no chat) that writes the 
sequences of the movie for each act
• Inputs per sequence
– Premise.json, arc.json – give agent global view of the movie
– All previous sequences generated so far – give agent local context 
for what should happen next
•
Primary sequence elements
– Goal – what is the narrative purpose of this sequence
– Key locations – locations in the sequence
– Exit beat – how the sequence ends and transitions to next sequence
• This agent then cre

– All previous sequences generated so far – give agent local context

slides.summary:
Scene Agent
• An automated agent (no chat) that writes the scenes 
of the movie for each sequence
• Inputs per sequence
– Premise.json, arc.json, sequence.json – give agent global view of 
the movie
– All scenes generated so far in this sequence and the previous 
sequence – give agent local context for what should happen next
•
Primary scene elements
– Slug line – location of the scene
– Blocks – series of actions and dialogues in the scene
• This agent then creates a file scenes.json
bullet_points: • An automated agent (no chat) that writes the scenes
of the movie for each sequence
– Premise.json, arc.json, sequence.json – give agent global view of

slides.summary:
Enhancement Agents
•
We can improve the agentic flow 
by using agents to enhance 
intermediate outputs
– Write better sequences or scenes
– Clean up the premise
– Make the arc more creative
bullet_points: We can improve the agentic flow
slides.relation_to_previous: This slide extends the prior discussion anchored in “Slide 15”, carrying forward the ideas around: Scenes.json.

slides.summary:

used for other long text documents
